summary: Tested the order cancellation flow end-to-end: backend endpoints, Guest Cancel page (/cancel-order), Admin OrdersManager cancel button + reason textarea, and Profile orders cancel button + reason textarea. All 9 backend pytest tests passed (9/9). All 3 frontend UI flows verified visually with screenshots.
critical_code_review_comments: orders.py line 495: `ObjectId(order_id) if len(order_id) == 24 else None` inside a $or list — when length != 24, the clause becomes {'_id': None}, harmless but messy. Consider building the $or list dynamically.
Backend /api/orders/guest/{id}/cancel reads token from query_params manually instead of declaring it as a FastAPI Query parameter — works but loses OpenAPI documentation visibility.
Profile.jsx 'Cancel Order' button shows for any order not in shipped/delivered/cancelled status; client-side gating is correctly mirrored on backend (status check).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (3/3 flows: Guest cancel page, Admin cancel form, Profile cancel form)

seed_data_creation: No seed data created. Tested against existing 18 admin-side orders.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Order cancellation feature is fully working. GuestCancelOrder.jsx route /cancel-order?order=X&token=Y is wired correctly. Admin OrdersManager shows 'Откажи поръчката' which expands an inline form with title 'Защо отказвате тази поръчка?' + textarea + 'Потвърди отказ'/'Назад' buttons. Profile.jsx shows 'Cancel Order'/'Отказ от поръчка' button for pending/confirmed/processing orders which expands a textarea with title 'Why do you want to cancel?' / 'Защо искате да откажете поръчката?'. Backend POST /api/orders/{id}/cancel, /api/orders/guest/{id}/cancel?token=X, and /api/orders/admin/{id}/cancel all accept {reason: str} body and behave correctly (401/403 without auth, 404 for missing order, 400 for missing token, 422 for missing reason). To test the happy path of an actual cancellation, a real order with a known cancellation_token would need to be seeded.
